description: Close all gaps identified in docs/tasks/001-gap-analysis.md: replace raw SQL with the getParam() API, fix the i18n key mismatch, add a day-of-week guard to prevent weekend crashes and null color_id writes, add Saturday/Sunday and configurable timezone support, apply PSR-12 throughout, add PHPDoc, tests, and CI.

userStories.title: Replace raw SQL helpers with getParam() API
userStories.description: Delete the private methods `projectHasCustomColors()` and `getColorSettings()`. Replace the entire `getColorForDay()` implementation with direct `$this->getParam()` calls — ActionManager already populates `$this->params` for the current action instance before `execute()` is called, so no DB queries are needed. Confirm no raw SQL string interpolation remains anywhere in the action class.
acceptanceCriteria: `projectHasCustomColors()` method is removed
`getColorSettings()` method is removed
`getColorForDay()` (or its inline replacement) reads colors via `$this->getParam($day)` with no SQL queries
No `$this->db->getConnection()->query(...)` calls remain in the action class
`use PDO` import is removed (no longer needed)
`ralphi check` passes (lint + phpcs)
userStories.notes: GAP-01 (SQL injection), GAP-04 (bypasses getParam API). Confirmed via Kanboard source: ActionManager::attachEvents() calls setParam($name, $value) for every action_has_params row for the specific action_id before registering the listener.

userStories.title: Fix i18n parameter key strategy — use fixed English keys
userStories.description: Change `getActionRequiredParameters()` to use fixed English strings as keys instead of `t('Monday')` etc. The translated day names are stored as `action_has_params.name`; using `t()` means non-English installations store e.g. 'Lundi' but the day-of-week lookup always produces English names from `DateTime::format('l')`, causing a permanent mismatch. Switch to fixed English keys on both sides so the lookup is always consistent regardless of locale. Document this as a breaking change for any existing non-English configurations.
acceptanceCriteria: `getActionRequiredParameters()` returns keys `'Monday'`, `'Tuesday'`, `'Wednesday'`, `'Thursday'`, `'Friday'` (plain strings, no `t()` wrapper on the keys)
The day-of-week lookup uses the same plain English strings (matching `DateTime::format('l')` output)
A code comment explains why `t()` is intentionally not used on parameter keys
CHANGELOG.md updated noting the breaking change for non-English installations
`ralphi check` passes
userStories.notes: GAP-03 (i18n failure). DateTime::format('l') always returns English day names regardless of PHP locale. Storing translated names as param keys creates an unresolvable mismatch. Fixed English keys on both sides is the simplest correct approach.

userStories.title: Add day-of-week guard to hasRequiredCondition()
userStories.description: Add a guard in `hasRequiredCondition()` that resolves the day of week from the `date_due` timestamp and checks that `$this->getParam($day)` is not null. This prevents `doAction()` from being called for days with no configured color (currently: weekends), closing the silent null `color_id` corruption bug. Remove the redundant `projectHasCustomColors()` check (already deleted in US-001). `doAction()` becomes unconditional — it can trust the condition fully guards every failure path.
acceptanceCriteria: `hasRequiredCondition()` resolves the day of week from `date_due` and returns `false` when `$this->getParam($day)` is null or empty
`hasRequiredCondition()` no longer calls `projectHasCustomColors()` (removed in US-001)
`doAction()` contains no conditional logic — it calls the color lookup and update unconditionally
Tasks with a Saturday or Sunday due date: `hasRequiredCondition()` returns `false`, task color is unchanged
Tasks with a weekday due date and configured color: `hasRequiredCondition()` returns `true`, color is assigned correctly
`ralphi check` passes
userStories.notes: GAP-02 (weekend crash), GAP-05 (no day guard). Closes the null color_id corruption path. doAction() should be a clean unconditional call per Kanboard action lifecycle conventions.

userStories.title: Add Saturday and Sunday color parameters
userStories.description: Add `'Saturday'` and `'Sunday'` to `getActionRequiredParameters()` so users can configure weekend colors. Provide a sentinel 'no change' option — an empty string value or a dedicated 'none' entry in the color list — so users can leave weekend tasks uncolored. Update the day-of-week guard in `hasRequiredCondition()` (US-003) to treat the sentinel value as a skip (return false), preserving the 'do nothing on weekends' default.
acceptanceCriteria: `getActionRequiredParameters()` returns entries for all seven days: Monday through Sunday
A 'no change' / 'none' sentinel option is available in the color selection for each day
`hasRequiredCondition()` returns `false` when the configured value for the resolved day is the sentinel
Tasks with a Saturday due date and a color configured for Saturday: color is assigned correctly
Tasks with a Saturday due date and 'none' configured for Saturday: color is unchanged
CHANGELOG.md updated under [Unreleased]
`ralphi check` passes
userStories.notes: GAP-07 (no Sat/Sun support). Closes the weekend feature gap opened up once GAP-02/05 are fixed.

userStories.title: Add configurable timezone action parameter
userStories.description: Replace the hardcoded `'America/New_York'` timezone with a new action parameter `'Timezone'` that defaults to the PHP server timezone (`date_default_timezone_get()`). Add `'Timezone'` to `getActionRequiredParameters()` with an appropriate input type (free-text string or a predefined list of common IANA timezone identifiers). Use the configured timezone when constructing the `DateTime` object for day-of-week resolution.
acceptanceCriteria: `getActionRequiredParameters()` includes a `'Timezone'` parameter
No hardcoded timezone string remains in the action class
Day-of-week resolution uses the timezone from `$this->getParam('Timezone')`
When the Timezone parameter is empty or null, falls back to `date_default_timezone_get()`
CHANGELOG.md updated under [Unreleased]
`ralphi check` passes
userStories.notes: GAP-06 (hardcoded timezone). Independent of US-004; both depend on US-003 only.

userStories.title: PSR-12 indentation, phantom import, and strict comparison fixes
userStories.description: Mechanical style-only pass. (1) Run PHP-CS-Fixer with `--rules=@PSR12` to normalize all indentation to 4 spaces — both files currently mix tabs and spaces. (2) Remove `use Kanboard\Model\ColorModel` (phantom import — `$this->colorModel` is resolved via the DI container magic `__get`, not this `use` statement) and add a `@property \Kanboard\Model\ColorModel $colorModel` annotation to the class PHPDoc. (3) Change the loose `==` to `===` in the `hasRequiredCondition()` `color_id` comparison.
acceptanceCriteria: All PHP files use 4-space indentation with no tab characters
`use Kanboard\Model\ColorModel` is removed from the action class
A `@property \Kanboard\Model\ColorModel $colorModel` annotation is present on the class (added as part of US-007 PHPDoc, or added here if US-007 has not run yet)
The `color_id` comparison in `hasRequiredCondition()` uses `===`
`ralphi check` passes (phpcs reports zero PSR-12 violations)
userStories.notes: GAP-08 (mixed indentation), GAP-10 (phantom import), GAP-11 (loose ==). Sequenced after US-003 so the style pass runs on stabilised code.

userStories.title: Add PHPDoc blocks to all classes and methods
userStories.description: Add class-level and method-level PHPDoc to both `Plugin.php` and `Action/AssignColorsByDayOfWeek.php`. Each class needs a `@package` and `@author` tag. Each method needs a one-line description, `@param` with types and names, and `@return` with type. Include a `@property` annotation for `$colorModel` on the action class (if not already added in US-006).
acceptanceCriteria: All public and private methods in both files have PHPDoc blocks with `@param`, `@return`, and a description
Both classes have class-level PHPDoc with `@package` and `@author`
Action class has `@property \Kanboard\Model\ColorModel $colorModel` on the class PHPDoc
PHPStan level-5 reports no new errors introduced
`ralphi check` passes
userStories.notes: GAP-09 (no PHPDoc). Sequenced after US-006 so the style pass has already normalized indentation.

userStories.title: Clarify date_due semantics in description and documentation
userStories.description: The plugin name and short description say 'by day of week' without specifying which day. Users reasonably expect colors to reflect the creation day; the implementation uses the due date's day. Update `getDescription()` to say 'based on the day of the week of the task\'s due date'. Update `README.md` usage section and `docs/001-architecture.md` data flow section to make the due-date basis explicit.
acceptanceCriteria: `getDescription()` return value explicitly mentions 'due date'
`README.md` usage walkthrough clarifies that color is determined by the due date's day of week
`docs/001-architecture.md` data flow description is updated to reflect due-date basis
`docs/001-architecture.md` front-matter `date_modified` is bumped to today
CHANGELOG.md updated under [Unreleased]
`ralphi check` passes
userStories.notes: GAP-12 (date_due semantics undocumented). Low-risk doc-only change, sequenced after core fixes for accuracy.

userStories.title: Add LICENSE file and fix plugin homepage URL
userStories.description: Add a standard MIT `LICENSE` file (author: Brad Campbell, year: 2026) — README.md already states MIT but no file exists. Update `getPluginHomepage()` in `Plugin.php` to return the repository URL instead of the personal site.
acceptanceCriteria: A `LICENSE` file exists at the repo root with standard MIT text, year 2026, and author Brad Campbell
`Plugin.php::getPluginHomepage()` returns the repository HTTPS URL
CHANGELOG.md updated under [Unreleased]
`ralphi check` passes
userStories.notes: GAP-15 (no LICENSE), GAP-16 (wrong homepage). Trivial independent tasks; grouped for efficiency.

userStories.title: Add composer.json and PHPUnit test suite
userStories.description: Create `composer.json` with `phpunit/phpunit` and `phpstan/phpstan` as dev dependencies, PSR-4 autoloading for the plugin namespace, and a `test` script. Create `tests/Action/AssignColorsByDayOfWeekTest.php` with unit tests covering the now-fixed behaviour: (1) `hasRequiredCondition()` returns true for weekday with configured color; (2) returns false for weekday with no configured color; (3) returns false for weekend day; (4) returns false for zero due date; (5) returns false when task has non-default color; (6) `doAction()` calls `taskModificationModel->update()` with the correct `color_id`.
acceptanceCriteria: `composer.json` exists with `phpunit/phpunit` and `phpstan/phpstan` dev dependencies and correct PSR-4 autoload mapping
`tests/Action/AssignColorsByDayOfWeekTest.php` exists and covers all six test cases listed above
`./vendor/bin/phpunit` exits 0 with all tests green
`./vendor/bin/phpstan analyse --level=5` exits 0 with no errors
`ralphi check` passes
userStories.notes: GAP-13 (no tests). Sequenced after US-003/004/005 so tests cover the fully corrected behaviour, not intermediate buggy states.

userStories.title: Add CI pipeline
userStories.description: Add a Gitea Actions (or GitHub Actions) CI workflow at `.gitea/workflows/ci.yml` (or `.github/workflows/ci.yml`) that runs on push and pull request to `main`. Pipeline stages: (1) PHP syntax check via `bash scripts/docker-lint.sh`; (2) Composer install; (3) PHPCS via `bash scripts/docker-phpcs.sh`; (4) PHPStan via `bash scripts/docker-phpstan.sh`; (5) PHPUnit via `bash scripts/docker-test.sh`.
acceptanceCriteria: A CI workflow file exists at `.gitea/workflows/ci.yml` or `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
Workflow triggers on push and pull_request to main
All five pipeline stages are defined: lint, composer install, phpcs, phpstan, phpunit
Workflow uses the kanboard/kanboard Docker image with entrypoint override for lint stage
CHANGELOG.md updated under [Unreleased]
`ralphi check` passes
userStories.notes: GAP-14 (no CI). Final story — depends on test suite (US-010) being in place first.
